Splinter faction of the Weather Underground that continued clandestine operations.

edges.evidence_quote: the group was organized as a faction of the national leadership of Students for a Democratic Society (SDS).
edges.context: The Weather Underground was originally a faction of SDS.

edges.evidence_quote: the SDS began to fragment and collapse following a split between the group's leadership in the 'National Office', the Progressive Labor Party (PLP) and their respective supporters.
edges.context: The Weather Underground faction split from the PLP within SDS.

edges.evidence_quote: The Weathermen strongly sympathized with the radical Black Panther Party; the police killing of Panther Fred Hampton prompted the Weathermen to issue a declaration of war upon the U.S. government.
edges.context: The Weather Underground sympathized with and was allied with the Black Panther Party.

edges.evidence_quote: Some members of the group joined the May 19th Communist Organization and continued their activities until that group disbanded in 1985.
edges.context: Former WUO members joined the May 19th Communist Organization after WUO dissolved.
The Black Liberation Army is listed as an ally of the Weather Underground in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Groups: Seattle Weather Collective , Women's Brigade
edges.context: The Seattle Weather Collective is a sub-group of the Weather Underground.

edges.evidence_quote: Groups: Seattle Weather Collective , Women's Brigade
edges.context: The Women's Brigade is a sub-group of the Weather Underground.

edges.evidence_quote: the group was organized as a faction of the national leadership of Students for a Democratic Society (SDS).
edges.context: Weather Underground originated as a faction within SDS.

edges.evidence_quote: the prominence of the Black Panther Party
edges.context: Weathermen saw the Black Panther Party as a revolutionary example.

edges.evidence_quote: the emergence of the Tupamaros organization in Uruguay
edges.context: Weathermen viewed Tupamaros as supporting the assertion of imminent worldwide revolution.

edges.evidence_quote: Charles Manson was an obsession within the group and Dohrn claimed he truly understood the iniquity of white America, with the Manson family being praised for the murder of Sharon Tate
edges.context: Weathermen praised the Manson Family for the Tate murder.

edges.evidence_quote: Women's groups such as The Motor City Nine and Cell 16 took the lead in various recruitment efforts.
Women's groups such as The Motor City Nine and Cell 16 took the lead in various recruitment efforts.
edges.context: Motor City Nine recruited members for Weatherman.

edges.evidence_quote: the decision was also taken to abolish Students for a Democratic Society. This decision reflected the splintering of SDS into hostile rival factions.
edges.context: Weather Underground leadership decided to abolish SDS, reflecting a hostile split.

edges.evidence_quote: the fire-bombing of Judge Murtagh's home, in solidarity with the Panther 21, was carried out by four members of the New York cell
edges.context: Weather Underground carried out an attack in solidarity with the Panther 21 members of the Black Panther Party.

edges.evidence_quote: the group accepted a $25,000 payment from the largest international psychedelic drug distribution organization, called The Brotherhood of Eternal Love, to break LSD advocate Timothy Leary out of a California prison
edges.context: Weather Underground accepted payment from The Brotherhood of Eternal Love to break Timothy Leary out of prison.

edges.evidence_quote: by the late 1970s, the Weatherman group had further split into two factions—the May 19th Communist Organization and the Prairie Fire Collective
edges.context: May 19th Communist Organization was a splinter faction of the Weather Underground.

edges.evidence_quote: by the late 1970s, the Weatherman group had further split into two factions—the May 19th Communist Organization and the Prairie Fire Collective
edges.context: Prairie Fire Collective was a splinter faction of the Weather Underground.

edges.evidence_quote: three former members, Kathy Boudin, Judith Alice Clark, and David Gilbert, joined the May 19 Communist Organization, and on October 20, 1981, in Nanuet, New York, the group helped the Black Liberation Army rob a Brink's armored truck
edges.context: May 19th Communist Organization allied with Black Liberation Army to commit the Brink's robbery.
